content: Hey Saturday, can you help me plan my Spanish study? I'm currently A2 and I want to hit B2 by the end of this year.

Absolutely—you’ve got a clear target, which is great. Going from A2 to B2 by year-end is doable with consistent daily input and speaking practice. Tell me what your current routine looks like and I’ll help you tighten it up.
I’m studying with Isabella Romero once a week for a 1-hour conversation session. I also do Anki every day: 50 new cards and around 200 reviews, plus 30 minutes of Spanish TV without subtitles.

That’s a strong routine already, especially the daily immersion and weekly speaking with Isabella Romero. I’d keep that structure but add one focused grammar block 4 days/week (20–25 min) so progress is more targeted. With your pace, B2 by December 31 is realistic if you track weak points weekly.
My biggest weak points are subjunctive and ser vs estar. I keep freezing when I try to speak.

Totally normal at A2—those two areas trip up almost everyone. Ask Isabella to do short roleplay drills where you must use triggers like “quiero que,” “es importante que,” and contrast sentences with ser/estar in context. Also, make a small Anki deck of your own mistakes from each session and review it daily.
That makes sense. Can you give me a simple weekly structure I can actually stick to?

Sure: keep daily Anki (50 new/200 reviews) + 30 min TV, then add Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri grammar practice focused on subjunctive and ser vs estar for 20 minutes. Use your 1-hour weekly session with Isabella Romero for speaking-only correction and save 10 minutes at the end to log recurring errors. On Sunday, do a 15-minute self-check: record yourself, note mistakes, and set one micro-goal for the next week.
